questions.prompt: Your subscription product has 38% recovery rate on failed payments — email-only dunning. What changes the most?

choices.text: Multi-channel recovery UX: email + in-app banner + reason-specific messaging + one-click Customer Portal link. Enable Card Account Updater (free) for silent card changes. Specific 'your card expired' beats generic 'payment failed.' Recovery rates routinely jump to 60-70% with this stack.

questions.explanation: Email-only is leaving recovery on the table. Most users miss the first email. Multi-channel + specific messaging + one-click fix + Stripe's free Card Account Updater can roughly double recovery rates. Recovery is your highest-ROI churn lever on already-paying customers.

questions.prompt: What's the right pattern for subscription feature gating during `past_due`?

choices.text: Keep features active during a grace period (typically 7-14 days). Pair with banner + email asking the user to fix billing. After Stripe's retries exhaust without resolution, then restrict features. Instant cutoff feels punitive; grace gives users time to fix what's usually a card issue, not intent to leave.
Cancel the subscription on first failure.

questions.explanation: Most failed payments are operational (card expired, momentary funds issue), not intentional churn. Grace period + clear recovery UX usually fixes it. Instant feature cutoff turns operational failures into actual churn.
